title: research_4.2.1
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Gross Cooling Effectiveness = (Tgas - Tbulk metal) / (Tgas - Tcoolant supply)
*   Heat Loading Parameter = (m coolant * Cp coolant) / (2 * H gas * A gas)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 4. Cooling Technology Performance Chart**
    *   **Axes:**
        *   y-axis: Gross Cooling Efficiency (0% to 100%)
        *   x-axis: Heat Loading Parameter (no units specified, implied dimensionless)
    *   **Curves:**
        *   Convection cooling (dashed line, lowest performance)
        *   Convection + Film cooling (dash-dot line, intermediate performance)
        *   Convection + Film cooling + TBC (solid line, highest performance)

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 5. Simplified to Complex Cooling Design Analysis**
    *   **Components and Labels:**
        *   Airfoil: Showing a 3D view degrading to a 2D and then 1D representation.
        *   2D Cross-section of the airfoil.
            *   Pressure Side
            *   Suction Side
            *   X=0 coordinate
        *   Hot Air: Arrow representing hot gas flow.
        *   Coolant Air: Arrow representing coolant flow.
        *   1D: Flat plate external flow; Internal cooling flow

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Tgas: average hot gas temperature
*   Tbulk metal: average metal temperature of entire airfoil with endwalls
*   Tcoolant supply: temperature of coolant entering the airfoil
*   Hgas: average external gas heat transfer coefficient (corrected for radiation)
*   Agas: external gas wetted surface area
*   m coolant: coolant flow rate to airfoil
*   Cp coolant: coolant specific heat

title: research_4.2.1
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
**1. Mathematical equations**

*   Nu = 0.023 Re^0.8 Pr^0.33
*   Nu = C * Re^n Pr^m
*   ηaw = C1 / (x/Ms)^n
*   ηaw = C1 / (x/Ms + C2)
*   ηaw = C1 Re^0.2 / (x/Ms)^0.8
*   ηaw = C1 / {1 + C2 (x/Ms)^0.8}
*   ηaw = (Trec - Taw) / (Trec - Trec coolant)
*   M = (ρV)coolant / (ρV)gas

**2. Graphs/charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters**

*   Re = film jet Reynolds number
*   M = (ρV)coolant / (ρV)gas = mass velocity ratio
*   s = equivalent film row slot width
*   x = distance
*   Ms = Mach number
*   Nu = Nusselt number
*   C, C1, C2 = Constants
*   Pr = Prandtl number
*   n, m = exponents
*   ηaw = Adiabatic film effectiveness
*   Trec = Recovery temperature
*   Taw = Adiabatic wall temperature

*   **Key Values:** (Approximate - From visual estimation of bar heights)

    *   External Heat Transfer Coefficient: Uncertainty ~ 35, Impact ~ 30
    *   Adiabatic Film Effectiveness: Uncertainty ~ 20, Impact ~ 15
    *   TBC Thermal Properties: Uncertainty ~ 12, Impact ~ 7
    *   Metal Thermal Properties: Uncertainty ~ 15, Impact ~ 10
    *   Internal Heat Transfer Coefficient: Uncertainty ~ 22, Impact ~ 17
    *   Internal Coolant Temperature: Uncertainty ~ 12, Impact ~ 8

title: research_4.2.1
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 11]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   A typical combustion system may use up to 7% of the available pressure from the compressor.
*   This cooling system pressure loss is roughly equivalent to 1% in cycle efficiency.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 11. Heavy Frame Turbine Secondary Flow Regions**
    *   Diagram shows flow regions within a turbine section. Key components are labelled as:
        *   Outboard air supplies
        *   Shrouds & hangers
        *   Nozzle diaphragm
        *   Lab seals
        *   Upper wheelspaces
        *   Lower wheelspaces
        * Inboard air supplies

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 11. Heavy Frame Turbine Secondary Flow Regions**
    *   **Components and Labels:**
        *   Outboard air supplies
        *   Shrouds & hangers
        *   Nozzle diaphragm
        *   Lab seals
        *   Upper wheelspaces
        *   Lower wheelspaces
        *   Inboard air supplies

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Combustor exit average turbulence intensity: up to 30%
*   Pressure loss due to cooling: Up to 7% of compressor pressure
*   Cycle efficiency loss related to 7% pressure loss: 1%

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Flow areas, lengths, restrictions, and discharge coefficients are critical for defining secondary flow models.
*   Friction coefficients and discharge coefficients are needed to determine boundary conditions.
*   Heat transfer coefficients and gas temperatures need to be known
